en.extract:
The turquoise-throated barbet (Psilopogon chersonesus) is an Asian barbet found in Thailand. The barbets get their name from the bristles which fringe their heavy bills; this species eats fruits and insects.  It used to be considered a subspecies of the blue-throated barbet.

The '''turquoise-throated barbet''' ('''''Psilopogon chersonesus''''') is an [[Megalaimidae|Asian barbet]] found in [[Thailand]]. The barbets get their name from the bristles which fringe their heavy bills; this species eats fruits and insects.  It used to be considered a subspecies of the [[blue-throated barbet]].
